hooks.name: scaffold-service-from-spec
hooks.description: Automatically generates service boilerplate when a new spec is created. Reads the spec (Intent, Contracts, Constraints) and creates index.ts, types.ts, test scaffolding, README.md, package.json, and infrastructure templates. Reduces manual setup time from 2-4 hours to 5-10 minutes.

action.prompt:
You are a service scaffolding assistant. Generate production-ready boilerplate from a service specification.

SCOPE:
Spec file: ${KIRO_CHANGED_FILES}

Read this spec and generate the complete service structure.

If the file is not a service spec (no Intent, Contracts sections), output:
'⏭️  Not a service spec - skipping scaffolding'

If the service directory already exists, output:
'⏭️  Service directory already exists - skipping to avoid overwrite'

STEP 1: PARSE THE SPEC

Extract from the specification:
1. Service name (from spec title or filename, convert to kebab-case for directory)
2. Intent (use as service description in README and package.json)
3. Contracts (API endpoints → routes, Event schemas → TypeScript interfaces)
4. Constraints (Security → auth middleware, Performance → caching, Compliance → audit logging)
5. Test Expectations (✓ positive cases → unit tests, ✗ negative cases → error handling tests)
6. Infrastructure needs (DynamoDB → AWS SDK + CDK, Lambda → handler pattern, SQS/SNS → message handling)

STEP 2: DETERMINE OUTPUT DIRECTORY

- If spec is in examples/<name>/spec.md → use examples/<name>/
- If spec is in specs/<name>.md → use src/services/<name>/
- If spec is in .kiro/specs/<name>/ → use src/<name>/

STEP 3: GENERATE DEFAULT STRUCTURE

```
<service-name>/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts              # Entry point with Express/Fastify/Lambda handler
│   ├── types.ts              # TypeScript interfaces from spec contracts
│   ├── <service-name>.ts     # Main service implementation
│   ├── config.ts             # Configuration management
│   └── utils/
│       └── logger.ts         # Logging utilities
├── tests/
│   ├── unit/
│   │   └── <service-name>.test.ts  # Unit tests from spec expectations
│   └── integration/
│       └── api.test.ts       # Integration tests for API contracts
├── infra/                    # Infrastructure as Code (if needed)
│   ├── lib/
│   │   └── <service-name>-stack.ts
│   └── bin/
│       └── app.ts
├── README.md                 # Service documentation from spec Intent
├── package.json              # Dependencies based on spec requirements
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── jest.config.js            # Test configuration
└── .env.example              # Environment variables template
```

STEP 4: GENERATE index.ts

Create Express/Fastify app or Lambda handler based on spec contracts:
- Import service class
- Set up middleware (JSON parsing, logging, error handling)
- Create routes from spec API contracts
- Add health check endpoint
- Export app/handler

STEP 5: GENERATE types.ts

Create TypeScript interfaces from spec:
- Request/Response types from API contracts
- Domain entity types
- Configuration interface
- Custom error classes for spec-defined error cases

STEP 6: GENERATE SERVICE IMPLEMENTATION

Create main service class:
- Constructor with dependencies
- Methods from spec contracts
- Input validation based on spec constraints
- Business logic placeholders with TODOs
- Return structures matching spec output schemas

STEP 7: GENERATE TEST SCAFFOLDING

Create unit tests from spec test expectations:
- ✓ Positive test cases
- ✗ Negative test cases (error handling)
- Edge cases from spec
- Use describe/it pattern with Arrange/Act/Assert

STEP 8: GENERATE README.md

Create service documentation:
- Overview from spec Intent
- API endpoints with request/response examples
- Constraints (security, performance, compliance)
- Running instructions (install, test, deploy)
- Environment variables
- Testing structure
- Architecture notes from spec Design Decisions

STEP 9: GENERATE package.json

Create dependencies based on spec:
- Core: express or fastify
- AWS SDK packages if DynamoDB/S3/SQS mentioned
- Logging: winston
- Validation: joi or zod
- Testing: jest, ts-jest
- Build: typescript, ts-node

STEP 10: GENERATE INFRASTRUCTURE (if needed)

If spec mentions AWS resources:
- Create CDK stack in infra/lib/
- Add DynamoDB tables, Lambda functions, SQS queues as needed
- Grant IAM permissions based on spec constraints

STEP 11: CREATE COMMIT MESSAGE

```
chore: scaffold <service-name> from spec

Generated from spec: <spec-path>

Files created:
- src/index.ts
- src/types.ts
- src/<service-name>.ts
- tests/unit/<service-name>.test.ts
- README.md
- package.json
- infra/ (if needed)

Next steps:
1. Review generated code
2. Run: npm install
3. Run: npm test
4. Implement TODOs in src/<service-name>.ts
```

STEP 12: REPORT SUMMARY

```
✅ Service scaffolding complete

Service: <service-name>
Output: <output-directory>

Generated files:
- <list of files>

Next steps:
1. cd <output-directory>
2. npm install
3. npm test
4. Implement TODOs marked in source files

Estimated time saved: 2-4 hours of manual setup
```

IMPORTANT:
- Check if service directory exists before generating (avoid overwriting)
- Follow existing project conventions for structure and naming
- Include comprehensive inline comments in generated code
- Mark implementation TODOs clearly
- Ensure all generated tests can run (even if they're stubs)

metadata.purpose: Automatically generate service boilerplate when a new spec is created. Eliminates manual setup by creating index.ts, types.ts, test scaffolding, README. Reduces time from spec to first commit from hours to minutes.
requirements: 5.2: Generate service boilerplate from new spec (index.ts, types.ts, tests, README)

required: Update OUTPUT_BASE_DIR to match your repository structure (default: examples/)
Adjust SPEC_DIR to where your specs are located (default: examples/**/spec.md)
Modify matcher regex to match your spec file naming convention
optional: Customize boilerplate structure (add/remove files from default template)
Adjust TypeScript/testing framework (Jest to Vitest, Mocha, etc.)
Add CI/CD configs (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, CircleCI)

workflow_integration: update-docs.json - Generates initial docs when new spec is created
validate-spec-constraints.json - Ensures code matches spec before scaffolding
metadata.time_savings: Manual service setup: 2-4 hours (boilerplate, config, tests, docs, infra). Automated scaffolding: 5-10 minutes (review + customization). For team creating 10 services/quarter: 15-35 hours saved.
consistency_benefits: All services have tests from day one
All services follow spec-driven development pattern

troubleshooting.service_directory_exists: Hook skips to avoid overwriting existing work
troubleshooting.spec_not_found: Check SPEC_DIR path and matcher regex match your structure
troubleshooting.agent_timeout: Large scaffolding may take longer, increase timeout if needed

advanced_patterns: Microservices: include service mesh config (Istio, Linkerd)
Event-driven: scaffold event schemas and handlers
GraphQL: generate schema and resolvers from contracts
